content: FAA STARS/ERAM (official FAA ATC order 7110.65 + display-standard research): data block minimum content is flight ID and altitude; Full Data Block (FDB) is shown when controller has separation responsibility for a correlated track; Limited Data Block (LDB) omits leader line, velocity vector, and position symbol. FAA's own research (NTL/DOT reports) documents that STARS and ERAM (formerly CARTS) were built independently and used inconsistent color coding for datablocks/targets/alerts historically, motivating later standardization work — i.e. there is no single universal industry color scheme, but red/flashing for conflict alerts and full/limited datablock distinction are consistent baseline conventions.
